skill.name: prompt-engineering
skill.description: Guides users through prompt engineering patterns for LLMs, image generators, and video models using inference.sh CLI examples.
skill.summary: Create clearer prompts for text, image, and video AI models.
skill.author: inference-sh

security_audit.summary: The static external-command findings are mostly Markdown examples for the inference.sh CLI, but the installer command at SKILL.md:21 is a true high-risk pipe-to-shell pattern. I found no evidence of weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, or prompt injection targeting the evaluator.

high_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped Directly To Shell
high_findings.description: The Quick Start instructs users to run a remote installer with curl and pipe it directly to sh before login. This can execute arbitrary code from the network without inspection. No evidence found that the skill author included a malicious payload in SKILL.md.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell pattern is present on line 21 and would execute remote content. The file does not prove malicious intent, so this is high risk rather than a confirmed block.

medium_findings.title: External AI CLI Commands Send Prompt Data To Providers
medium_findings.description: The skill contains many inference.sh CLI examples that send user prompts to external model providers. This appears to be the intended purpose, but users may expose sensitive prompt content if they paste private data into the examples.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly examples for an external AI CLI and are legitimate for this skill. The privacy risk is real because prompt text is transmitted to external services.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto And Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance hits occur in descriptive prompt-engineering text and example prompts. I found no cryptographic API use, no host enumeration command, and no evidence of hidden reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows natural-language examples, not executable crypto or reconnaissance code. The finding is still recorded to document the dismissal.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URL Is Mostly Benign
low_findings.description: The inference.sh URL is a normal product link in the guide. The same domain is used in the high-risk installer command, but the standalone documentation link does not create a security issue.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 16 is a Markdown link to the service used by the skill. It does not execute code or transmit data by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe To Shell Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a curl command piped into sh. This is dangerous because it executes network-delivered shell code without a local review step.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command syntax directly matches a high-risk remote shell execution pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly demonstrates infsh app run commands that submit prompt content to hosted model providers. This is expected behavior but should be disclosed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly invoke external model applications through the inference.sh CLI. The risk depends on user-provided prompt contents.

content.user_title: Improve AI Prompts Across Text, Image, and Video
content.value_statement: AI outputs become inconsistent when prompts lack context, constraints, or examples. This skill provides reusable prompt structures for Claude, Codex, image models, and video models.
actual_capabilities: Explains prompt structure for role, task, constraints, and output format.
Shows few-shot, chain-of-thought, system prompt, and structured output examples.
Provides prompt patterns for Claude, GPT-4, FLUX, Veo, and Stable Diffusion.
Demonstrates image prompt components such as subject, style, composition, and lighting.
Demonstrates video prompt components such as shot type, action, setting, and camera movement.
Includes reusable templates for code review, content writing, and image generation.
limitations: Examples rely on the inference.sh CLI and external model providers.
The guide does not validate model output quality automatically.
Prompt advice may need adjustment for private models or custom policies.
The install command uses a remote shell installer and should be reviewed before use.

use_cases.title: Create Reliable Assistant Prompts
use_cases.description: Build prompts with clear roles, tasks, constraints, and expected response formats.

use_cases.title: Prepare Creative Generation Briefs
use_cases.description: Write image and video prompts that specify subject, style, composition, lighting, and motion.

use_cases.title: Standardize Technical Review Prompts
use_cases.description: Use repeatable templates for code review, debugging, structured extraction, and prioritization tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Task Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a beginner needs a clear single-turn prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a prompt for [task]. Include the role, context, constraints, and output format. Keep the prompt short and practical.

prompt_templates.title: Few-Shot Prompt Builder
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when output style must match examples.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a few-shot prompt for [task]. Include two example inputs and outputs, then add a final input placeholder for my real request.

prompt_templates.title: Creative Model Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for image or video generation planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a generation prompt for [model type]. Specify the subject, setting, style, composition, lighting, quality terms, and negative prompt guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Prompt Optimization
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when an existing prompt needs diagnosis and refinement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this prompt for ambiguity, missing context, conflicting instructions, privacy risks, and weak output constraints. Then provide a revised prompt and explain each change.

output_examples.input: A vague request asking an AI to help with code.
output_examples.output: A revised prompt that identifies the programming language, expected behavior, actual behavior, constraints, and requested debugging format.

output_examples.input: A short image idea for a castle on a hill.
output_examples.output: A detailed image prompt with subject details, environment, lighting, style, composition, and quality descriptors.

output_examples.input: A business problem about cart abandonment.
output_examples.output: A multi-step prompting workflow that analyzes causes, ranks priorities, and proposes validation tests.

best_practices: State the task, audience, constraints, and output format before adding examples.
Remove private data before sending prompts to external model providers.
Iterate from a simple prompt to a detailed prompt and compare outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague requests when the output needs a specific format.
Do not combine conflicting style, length, or content instructions.
Do not run remote installer commands without inspecting the script source.

faq.question: What models does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers LLMs, image generators, and video models, including examples for Claude, GPT-4, FLUX, Veo, and Stable Diffusion.

faq.question: Does this skill generate prompts automatically?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and examples that help an AI assistant draft, review, and improve prompts.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance works for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when you adapt examples to your workflow.

faq.question: Does it require inference.sh?
faq.answer: The examples use inference.sh commands, but the prompt structures can be reused with other model interfaces.

faq.question: Is it safe to paste private data into examples?
faq.answer: No. Remove secrets, personal data, and confidential business details before using external model providers.

faq.question: Why is this skill marked high risk?
faq.answer: The guide includes a remote installer command piped to a shell. Users should inspect installers before running them.
